title: Use of English — Part 1: Multiple-choice cloze
description: Choose the best word (A, B, C or D) to complete each gap in the text. This tests your vocabulary knowledge.

questions.context: Wildlife photography has become increasingly popular in recent years. Many people are now (1) ___ their weekends exploring national parks with a camera.
questions.text: Question 1: Choose the correct word for gap (1).
questions.explanation: 'Spending time' is the correct collocation. We 'spend' time doing activities.

questions.context: The exhibition was a great (2) ___ for local artists, giving them a chance to display their work to a wide audience.
questions.text: Question 2: Choose the correct word for gap (2).
questions.explanation: 'Opportunity' collocates best with 'great' and implies a favourable set of circumstances.

questions.context: She had always (3) ___ an interest in marine biology, so a career studying ocean life seemed a natural choice.
questions.text: Question 3: Choose the correct word for gap (3).
questions.explanation: 'Shown an interest' is the standard collocation in British English.

questions.context: Despite the heavy rain, the outdoor concert went (4) ___ as planned and attracted thousands of fans.
questions.text: Question 4: Choose the correct word for gap (4).
questions.explanation: 'Go ahead' means to proceed or continue with a plan despite difficulties.

questions.context: The new library is (5) ___ reach of the town centre, so most students can get there by bicycle.
questions.text: Question 5: Choose the correct word for gap (5).
questions.explanation: 'Within reach' is the correct fixed phrase meaning 'close enough to be accessed'.

questions.context: The documentary (6) ___ light on the hidden dangers faced by deep-sea divers.
questions.text: Question 6: Choose the correct word for gap (6).
questions.explanation: 'Shed light on' is the correct phrasal idiom meaning to clarify or reveal something.

questions.context: Although the team had trained hard, they (7) ___ to win the championship after a difficult final match.
questions.text: Question 7: Choose the correct word for gap (7).
questions.explanation: 'Failed to win' means they did not succeed. 'Missed' needs an object; 'lost' needs 'the game'.

questions.context: It is worth (8) ___ in mind that the deadline for applications is the end of this month.
questions.text: Question 8: Choose the correct word for gap (8).
questions.explanation: 'Bear in mind' is the standard idiom meaning 'remember' or 'consider'.
